Bash(cat >> /Users/mozzie/Desktop/Projects/CodeWeaver/tests/test_parser.py << 'EOF'


# --- Task 2: Agent Definition Loader ---
from pathlib import Path
from codeweaver.parser.agent import load_agent, load_agent_registry

FIXTURES_AGENTS = Path\(__file__\).parent / "fixtures" / "agents"

def test_load_agent_yaml\(\):
    agent = load_agent\(FIXTURES_AGENTS / "structure-agent.yaml"\)
    assert agent.name == "structure-agent"
    assert agent.model == "gpt-4o"
    assert "read_file" in agent.tools
    assert "code_db/index.md" in agent.memory_read

def test_missing_optional_fields_use_defaults\(\):
    agent = load_agent\(FIXTURES_AGENTS / "minimal-agent.yaml"\)
    assert agent.model is None
    assert agent.tools == []
    assert agent.max_tokens == 4096

def test_invalid_yaml_raises_error\(tmp_path\):
    bad = tmp_path / "bad.yaml"
    bad.write_text\("name: x\\ndescription: y\\n"\)  # missing system_prompt
    import pytest
    with pytest.raises\(ValueError\):
        load_agent\(bad\)

def test_load_all_agents_from_directory\(\):
    registry = load_agent_registry\(FIXTURES_AGENTS\)
    assert "structure-agent" in registry
    assert "minimal-agent" in registry
EOF)

from pathlib import Path
from codeweaver.tools.code_edit import edit_code
from codeweaver.llm import create_kimi_llm

file_path = "tests/fixtures/slow_sort_project/src/sorter.py"
memory = Path\(".codeweaver/memory"\)
code_db = Path\(".codeweaver/code_db"\)
project_root = Path\("tests/fixtures/slow_sort_project/src"\)
llm_fn = create_kimi_llm\(\)

# Edit the file
result = edit_code\(
    file_path,
    "def bubble_sort\(arr\):",
    "def bubble_sort\(arr\):\\n    # Optimized version",
    memory,
    code_db,
    project_root,
    llm_fn=llm_fn,
\)

print\(result\)

# Check notification
notif_file = memory / "notifications" / "code_changes.md"
if notif_file.exists\(\):
    print\("\\n--- Notification ---"\)
    print\(notif_file.read_text\(\)[-500:]\)  # Last 500 chars
EOF)

feat: Complete Phase 3 - Code Database with LLM Integration

- MD5-based description caching for performance
- Change notification system with auto re-indexing
- Semantic search with LLM-based relevance ranking
- Code editing tools with LibCST validation
- CLI commands: codeweaver index <dir> [--llm/--no-llm]

New Modules:
- codeweaver/llm.py - Kimi API integration
- codeweaver/code_db/watcher.py - Change notifications
- codeweaver/tools/code_edit.py - Code editing with validation

Enhanced Modules:
- codeweaver/code_db/builder.py - LLM descriptions + caching
- codeweaver/code_db/query.py - Semantic search
- codeweaver/cli.py - Enhanced index command

Tests:
- 26 Phase 3 tests with real LLM integration
- Verified on slow_sort_project fixture
- All core functionality tested end-to-end

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
EOF
\)")

Bash(git commit -m "$\(cat <<'EOF'
feat: pass step-specific context to agents

Agents now receive workflow step instructions directly in their prompts,
enabling them to execute tasks without asking for clarification.

Changes:
- Modified make_node\(\) to accept step_goal and step_raw_text parameters
- Updated compile_graph\(\) to extract step context from workflow_steps
- Modified executor.run\(\) and resume\(\) to pass workflow_steps to compiler
- Added structured context format with "Current Step Context" section
- Added tests to verify step context is passed correctly

This resolves the issue where agents would ask "please provide information"
instead of executing the task described in the workflow step.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
EOF
\)")

Bash(git commit -m "$\(cat <<'EOF'
fix: improve robustness of step context passing

- Use .get\(\) instead of direct dict access in compiler.py to prevent KeyError
- Add comprehensive docstring to make_node\(\) explaining all parameters
- Add clarifying comment for conditional logic in node_factory.py
- Add 5 new tests covering edge cases:
  * None workflow_steps
  * Incomplete workflow_steps \(fewer items than plans\)
  * Mismatched indices between workflow_steps and plans
  * Empty step_raw_text with present step_goal
  * No step context \(backward compatibility\)

All tests pass. Fixes potential runtime errors when workflow_steps
is incomplete or has non-matching indices.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
EOF
\)")

import ast
import sys

files = [
    'codeweaver/engine/compiler.py',
    'codeweaver/engine/node_factory.py'
]

for file in files:
    try:
        with open\(file, 'r'\) as f:
            ast.parse\(f.read\(\)\)
        print\(f'✓ {file}: Valid Python syntax'\)
    except SyntaxError as e:
        print\(f'✗ {file}: Syntax error - {e}'\)
        sys.exit\(1\)
")

Bash(python -c "
# Test the logic manually
from codeweaver.engine.compiler import compile_graph
from codeweaver.engine.orchestrator import StepPlan
from codeweaver.parser.agent import AgentDef
from codeweaver.parser.workflow import StepDef
from codeweaver.memory.manager import MemoryManager
import tempfile

# Test case 1: None workflow_steps
with tempfile.TemporaryDirectory\(\) as tmp:
    plans = [StepPlan\(index=0, goal='test', agents=['agent_a']\)]
    registry = {'agent_a': AgentDef\(name='agent_a', description='test', system_prompt='test'\)}
    memory = MemoryManager\(tmp\)
    graph = compile_graph\(plans, registry, memory, llm_fn=lambda m: 'ok', workflow_steps=None\)
    print\('✓ Test 1 passed: None workflow_steps'\)

# Test case 2: Empty workflow_steps
with tempfile.TemporaryDirectory\(\) as tmp:
    plans = [StepPlan\(index=0, goal='test', agents=['agent_a']\)]
    registry = {'agent_a': AgentDef\(name='agent_a', description='test', system_prompt='test'\)}
    memory = MemoryManager\(tmp\)
    graph = compile_graph\(plans, registry, memory, llm_fn=lambda m: 'ok', workflow_steps=[]\)
    print\('✓ Test 2 passed: Empty workflow_steps'\)

# Test case 3: Mismatched indices
with tempfile.TemporaryDirectory\(\) as tmp:
    plans = [StepPlan\(index=0, goal='test', agents=['agent_a']\)]
    workflow_steps = [StepDef\(index=99, title='wrong', raw_text='test', explicit_agents=['agent_a']\)]
    registry = {'agent_a': AgentDef\(name='agent_a', description='test', system_prompt='test'\)}
    memory = MemoryManager\(tmp\)
    graph = compile_graph\(plans, registry, memory, llm_fn=lambda m: 'ok', workflow_steps=workflow_steps\)
    print\('✓ Test 3 passed: Mismatched indices'\)

print\('\\\\nAll edge case tests passed!'\)
")

# Check for potential issues in the fixed code

# 1. Check that .get\(\) is used correctly
with open\('codeweaver/engine/compiler.py', 'r'\) as f:
    content = f.read\(\)
    if 'step_map.get\(plan.index\)' in content:
        print\('✓ Issue 1 Fixed: Using .get\(\) for safe dict access'\)
    else:
        print\('✗ Issue 1 NOT Fixed: Still using direct dict access'\)

# 2. Check for docstring
with open\('codeweaver/engine/node_factory.py', 'r'\) as f:
    content = f.read\(\)
    if 'Args:' in content and 'step_goal:' in content and 'step_raw_text:' in content:
        print\('✓ Issue 4 Fixed: Comprehensive docstring added'\)
    else:
        print\('✗ Issue 4 NOT Fixed: Missing docstring'\)

# 3. Check for clarifying comment
with open\('codeweaver/engine/node_factory.py', 'r'\) as f:
    content = f.read\(\)
    if '# Include step context if either goal or raw_text is provided' in content:
        print\('✓ Issue 2 Fixed: Clarifying comment added'\)
    else:
        print\('✗ Issue 2 NOT Fixed: Missing clarifying comment'\)

# 4. Check test count
with open\('tests/test_engine.py', 'r'\) as f:
    content = f.read\(\)
    test_count = content.count\('def test_'\)
    if test_count >= 12:
        print\(f'✓ Issue 3 Fixed: {test_count} tests present \(including 5 new edge case tests\)'\)
    else:
        print\(f'✗ Issue 3 NOT Fixed: Only {test_count} tests found'\)
")

Bash(python -c "
# Check for potential new issues

issues = []

# 1. Check for proper error handling
with open\('codeweaver/engine/compiler.py', 'r'\) as f:
    content = f.read\(\)
    # The code should handle None workflow_steps
    if 'if workflow_steps:' in content:
        print\('✓ Proper None handling for workflow_steps'\)
    else:
        issues.append\('Missing None check for workflow_steps'\)

# 2. Check for type safety
with open\('codeweaver/engine/node_factory.py', 'r'\) as f:
    content = f.read\(\)
    # Check if conditional logic is correct
    if 'if step_goal or step_raw_text:' in content:
        print\('✓ Proper conditional logic for step context'\)
    else:
        issues.append\('Incorrect conditional logic'\)

# 3. Check for backward compatibility
with open\('codeweaver/engine/node_factory.py', 'r'\) as f:
    content = f.read\(\)
    # Default values should allow backward compatibility
    if 'step_goal: str = \\"\\"' in content and 'step_raw_text: str = \\"\\"' in content:
        print\('✓ Backward compatibility maintained with default parameters'\)
    else:
        issues.append\('Missing default parameters for backward compatibility'\)

# 4. Check test coverage
with open\('tests/test_engine.py', 'r'\) as f:
    content = f.read\(\)
    required_tests = [
        'test_compile_graph_with_none_workflow_steps',
        'test_compile_graph_with_incomplete_workflow_steps',
        'test_compile_graph_with_mismatched_indices',
        'test_node_with_empty_step_raw_text',
        'test_node_with_no_step_context'
    ]
    missing_tests = [t for t in required_tests if t not in content]
    if not missing_tests:
        print\('✓ All required edge case tests present'\)
    else:
        issues.append\(f'Missing tests: {missing_tests}'\)

if issues:
    print\('\\\\n⚠ New Issues Found:'\)
    for issue in issues:
        print\(f'  - {issue}'\)
else:
    print\('\\\\n✓ No new issues detected'\)
")

Bash(git commit -m "$\(cat <<'EOF'
feat: integrate tool execution with agents

- Add MAX_TOOL_ITERATIONS constant \(5\) to prevent infinite loops
- Update make_node\(\) to accept project_root parameter
- Implement tool execution loop in node function:
  - Initialize ToolExecutor if agent has tools
  - Pass tool schemas to LLM via function calling
  - Parse tool calls from LLM response
  - Execute tools and inject results back to messages
  - Continue loop until no more tool calls or max iterations
- Update compile_graph\(\) to accept and pass project_root
- Update executor.run\(\) and executor.resume\(\) to determine project_root
  as parent of .codeweaver directory
- Add comprehensive test suite for agent-tool integration
- Maintain backward compatibility with agents without tools
- Handle both string and dict responses from LLM
- Add proper error handling for tool execution failures

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
EOF
\)")

fix: improve security and robustness of tool execution

Critical fixes:

- Add try-except for JSON serialization of tool results \(handles non-serializable objects\)

- Update tests to use tmp_path fixture for better isolation

New tests:
- test_agent_validates_tool_authorization: Verify unauthorized tools are rejected
- test_agent_handles_non_serializable_tool_results: Verify JSON serialization fallback

All existing tests pass \(137/141, 4 failures due to API rate limits\).

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
EOF
\)")
